diff --git a/CEO_NOTES.md b/CEO_NOTES.md
index cbe2ec6..7f4f6e3 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -17,2 +17,17 @@
   - Started R&D tier 1; sampled completion day 30, duration 30 days, expected quality boost +0.0289, immediate cost $166,667.
 - Watch next week: ad leads by channel/group, accepted plan mix, compute margin especially S3, social reaction, any enterprise inbox. If S2 conversion is weak, consider pausing S2 ads until quality rises; if S3 is negative margin, tighten C quota or reduce S3 ads.
+
+## Week 2 / Day 7
+
+- Cash: $708,209. Week 1 produced 902 individual leads but only 3 subscribers; 899 leads were lost. Active subscribers: 2 S1 from search ads, 1 S3 from content marketing, all on A.
+- Channel lead yields were good but quality/offer fit was poor: content S3 216 leads, social S1 205, content S2 169, social S3 139, search S1 131, search S2 42.
+- Social posts complained about quota limits and quality, especially S3/S1. No enterprise inbox.
+- Pivot:
+  - Prices: A $19, B $99, C $189.
+  - Model tiers: A tier 3, B tier 3, C tier 4.
+  - Quotas raised to A 150, B 600, C 1200.
+  - Spend remains $15,000/day total dev but reallocated: global $5,000/day, targeted S1 $3,000, S2 $4,000, S3 $3,000.
+  - Ads cut to $300/day measurement budget: search S1 $100, content S2 $50, content S3 $150.
+  - Lead promos: S1 $5, S2 $40, S3 $20.
+  - Replied to S3 quota complaint with the quota increase and targeted quality work.
+- Watch next week: whether conversion improves after higher A quality and quotas, whether S2/S3 choose B/C, and whether higher quotas create compute-margin problems. If conversion remains weak, cut ads to zero and consider another week of focused dev before scaling acquisition.
